sections.paragraph:
προχωρέω (Soph., Hdt. et al.; ins, pap, TestIss 1:11) prim. sense ‘go forward, advance’, in our lit. only fig. to advance as state or condition, turn out, succeed w. dat. of pers. for whom someth. goes well (Hdt. 5, 62; Thu. 4, 73, 4; Lucian, Icar. 10; PLond 358, 13 [II a.d.] προχωρεῖν αὐτοῖς τὰ ἄνομα; Jos., Vi. 122) οὐδὲν αὐτοῖς ὅλως προχωρεῖ nothing at all turns out well for them Hs 6, 3, 5.—DELG s.v. χώρα.

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

TestIss TestIss = Testament of Issachar, s. Test12Patr—List 2

prim. prim. = primarily

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

dat. dat. = dative

pers. pers. = person(s)

someth. someth. = something

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 892
